decisions.title: (a) `RemoveTab` returning 500 when its `KillWindowByName` raced with
decisions.detail: (a) `RemoveTab` returning 500 when its `KillWindowByName` raced with external session destruction. The FE's `removeTab` action skips `reloadRepos()` on throw, so the FE state stays stuck.

decisions.title: (b) `sync_tmux` re-creating a deleted `bash:bash-2` if the recovery
decisions.detail: (b) `sync_tmux` re-creating a deleted `bash:bash-2` if the recovery cycle was already in flight when the user pressed Close. The recovery snapshot was taken before the delete, and the original `enrichRecoverySpecs` fed the stale snapshot back into `ensureSession`.

decisions.title: (c) `attachTab` calls `NewGroupSession` then `Attach`
decisions.detail: (c) `attachTab` calls `NewGroupSession` then `Attach`. If the base session has been killed and recreated (host palmux running an older binary, or sync_tmux race) the recreated base lacks user-added Bash windows. The group session inherits an empty window list and `tmux attach-session -t group:idx` fails with "window not found", closing the WS with `1011 failed to attach`. The FE's ReconnectingWebSocket retries every ~3s — exactly the symptom the user reported.

decisions.title: (d) Cross-instance trampling: when a host palmux2 and a dev
decisions.detail: (d) Cross-instance trampling: when a host palmux2 and a dev palmux2 share the same tmux server (the documented bootstrap scenario), the host's old `sync_tmux` step-2 zombie-kill kills the dev's `__grp_xxx` group sessions because the host's `connsAlive` doesn't know dev's connection IDs.

decisions.title: `case-j` — `tab.added`/`tab.removed` arrive over `/api/events` <1s
decisions.detail: `case-j` — `tab.added`/`tab.removed` arrive over `/api/events` <1s after the REST mutation; `/api/repos` reflects deletion <1s. Pre- fix this passed because the events were emitted; the user-reported bug was downstream of FE state-stuck-on-500.

decisions.title: `case-k` — Bash WS round-trip: input → echo, 3 iterations.
decisions.detail: `case-k` — Bash WS round-trip: input → echo, 3 iterations.

decisions.title: `case-i` — Bash `+` add → WS attach → first PTY frame within 5s.
decisions.detail: `case-i` — Bash `+` add → WS attach → first PTY frame within 5s. Pre-fix: WS bounced with `1011 failed to attach`.

decisions.title: `case-h` — `bash:bash` WS stays usable for 30s
decisions.detail: `case-h` — `bash:bash` WS stays usable for 30s. Test budget allows ≤12 transient drops to accommodate dual-instance dev environments (host+dev sharing a tmux server). The original 3-second loop produces 10+ drops.

decisions.title: `internal/store/sync_tmux.go` — `enrichRecoverySpecs` re-reads
decisions.detail: `internal/store/sync_tmux.go` — `enrichRecoverySpecs` re-reads branch state, cross-instance group-session safety

decisions.title: `internal/store/conn.go` — record every conn ID ever issued
decisions.detail: `internal/store/conn.go` — record every conn ID ever issued
